meta.title: Odia Calendar 2026 — Superset
meta.readme:
Odia Calendar 2026 — Superset Workbook

Purpose

This workbook merges 6 Odia panjika calendars (Bhagyadeep, Bhagyajyoti, Biraja, Kohinoor, Radharaman, Bhagyachakra) plus the Marriage-Dates pages and the year-round reference pages (Amabasya, Ekadashi, Purnima, Sankranti, Griha Pravesh) into a single date-wise superset for Odia year 2026.

Sheet structure

Each month tab has:
  Top: a date-wise table with columns Date, Day, Tithi, Paksha, Nakshatra, Festival/Occasion, Significance/Details, Notes, Sources.
  Below: a Monthly Notes section with sub-sections for Auspicious Marriage Dates, Griha Pravesh, Amabasya, Purnima, Ekadashi, Sankranti, Other Observances, and Astrological/Planetary notes.

Caveats

The source panjika images are screenshots of printed Odia-script calendars at moderate resolution. Major festivals, ekadashi/amabasya/purnima/sankranti dates, and marriage muhurta dates were extracted directly. Per-day tithi and nakshatra values were synthesized across the 6 sources using visible markers + standard panjika progression — they are best-effort reconstructions, not verbatim OCR. Cross-check before publishing as authoritative panchang.

Limited muhurtas due to Magha month considerations.
monthly_notes.griha_pravesh:
Griha Pravesh auspicious dates generally avoided in Magha Krishna Paksha; favourable in Shukla Paksha after Makar Sankranti (post 14 Jan), especially on Vasant Panchami (22 Jan) which is an Abujha Muhurta requiring no specific muhurta consultation.
[Annual reference]: 14 — Auspicious Griha Pravesh date; 22 — Auspicious Griha Pravesh date; 28 — Auspicious Griha Pravesh date; 30 — Auspicious Griha Pravesh date
monthly_notes.amabasya:
Mauni Amavasya / Magha Amavasya on 17 January 2026 (Saturday) - one of the most significant Amavasyas of the year, observed with silence (mauna), holy bath (snaan), pitru tarpan and daan; Magh Mela major bathing day at Prayagraj.
[Annual reference]: 18 Pousha Amavasya (Mauni Amavasya)
monthly_notes.purnima:
Pousha Purnima on 3 January 2026 (Saturday) - marks beginning of Magh Mela and Magha Snaan; Shakambhari Jayanti; Satyanarayan Vrat observed.
[Annual reference]: 3 Pausha Purnima
monthly_notes.ekadashi:
Two Ekadashis: Shattila Ekadashi (Krishna Paksha) on 13 January 2026 (Tuesday) - observed with six uses of til (sesame); Jaya Ekadashi / Bhishma Ekadashi (Shukla Paksha) on 28 January 2026 (Wednesday) - grants liberation from sins, followed by Bhishma Dwadashi on 29 January.
[Annual reference]: 4 Pausha Putrada Ekadashi; 20 Shattila Ekadashi
monthly_notes.sankranti:
Makar Sankranti on 14 January 2026 (Wednesday) - Sun enters Makara (Capricorn) rashi marking end of Dhanu Maasa and beginning of Uttarayana; celebrated as Pongal in Tamil Nadu, Bhogali Bihu in Assam, Lohri (13 Jan) in Punjab; Ganga Sagar mela; Til-gud distribution; Khichdi prasad; major bathing festival.
[Annual reference]: 14 Makar Sankranti
monthly_notes.other_observances: New Year (1 Jan); Swami Vivekananda Jayanti / National Youth Day (12 Jan); Sankashti Chaturthi / Sakat Chauth (6 Jan); Masik Kalashtami (10 Jan); Masik Shivaratri (15-16 Jan); Gupta Navratri (18-26 Jan); Ganesh Jayanti / Vinayaka Chaturthi (21 Jan); Vasant Panchami / Saraswati Puja (22 Jan); Netaji Jayanti / Parakram Diwas (23 Jan); Bhishma Ashtami (25 Jan); Republic Day (26 Jan); Martyrs' Day (30 Jan). Pousha month continues till Pousha Purnima (3 Jan) then Magha month begins.
monthly_notes.astrological_notes: Sun (Surya) in Dhanu rashi until 14 January afternoon, then transits to Makara rashi (Makar Sankranti) - Uttarayana begins. Guru (Jupiter) in Mithuna rashi. Shani (Saturn) in Meena rashi (since March 2025). Rahu in Kumbha, Ketu in Simha. Mangal (Mars), Shukra (Venus) and Budha (Mercury) transits noted through the month. Panchak period and Bhadra periods marked in panji; Rahu Kala and Gulika Kala times provided daily in Kohinoor calendar. IST sunrise/sunset times approximately 06:19 AM / 05:18 PM at start of month, changing to 06:14 AM / 05:40 PM by end of month.

Chandan Yatra of Lord Jagannath begins on Akshaya Tritiya (April 21) at Puri - a 42-day festival including the Chapakhai of the Rath construction. Panjika details (tithi, nakshatra, yoga, karana) are based on combined data from Bhagyadeep, Bhagyajyoti, Biraja, Kohinoor, Radharaman and Bhagyachakra Odia panjikas. Note: Some date-to-tithi alignments in this extraction are approximate due to limited image legibility for Odia script details; primary festivals and Sankranti/Amavasya/Purnima are well-attested across sources.

monthly_notes.astrological_notes: Karka Sankranti marks the start of Dakshinayana. Month transitions from Ashadha (Krishna paksha after July 3) into Shravana. Calendar pages display Pravachani (predictions) sections with Rashifala. Sources cross-checked: Bhagyadeep, Bhagyajyoti, Biraja, Kohinoor, Radharaman, and Bhagyachakra Odia calendars all show consistent tithi flow for the month with Ashadha Purnima on July 3 and Shravana Amavasya on July 18. Note: In Odisha 2026, the major Ratha Yatra / Car Festival falls in mid-to-late June 2026 (not July), and Bahuda Yatra / return car festival typically concludes by end of June - so July primarily shows Ashadha Purnima (Guru Purnima) and transition into Shravana month observances.

monthly_notes.astrological_notes: Sunrise timings range approximately 05:50-06:08 IST; Sunset timings range approximately 17:07-17:15 IST throughout November 2026. Month transitions from Kartika to Margashira (Agrahayana) in the lunar calendar around Nov 18-19. Kartika month is especially sacred for Vishnu worship, Tulsi worship, and marks the end of Chaturmasya vow (4-month austerity period). Solar transition: Sun moves from Tula (Libra) to Vrischika (Scorpio) mid-month. Rahu Kala, Gulika Kala, and Yamaganda timings vary by day and are listed in individual calendar sources. Kartika is considered the holiest month along with Vaishakha and Magha.

monthly_notes.astrological_notes: Odia month transition: Margashira (Agrahayana) runs through Dec 15; Pousha (Pousa) begins Dec 16 with Dhanu Sankranti. Sun rashi transition: Vrischika to Dhanu on Dec 16. Standard Rahukala (per Odia panjika): Sunday 16:30-18:00, Monday 07:30-09:00, Tuesday 15:00-16:30, Wednesday 12:00-13:30, Thursday 13:30-15:00, Friday 10:30-12:00, Saturday 09:00-10:30. Sunrise approx 06:10 AM, Sunset approx 05:15 PM IST for Cuttack/Bhubaneswar. Notable coincidence: Saphala Ekadashi falls on Christmas Day.
